summary.intent: Migrate the AI metrics dashboard from dark-themed Astro components to MUI React components with a light theme, fix the unreadable token breakdown chart (cached tokens dominating the visualization), and rename 'checkpoint' terminology to 'commit' throughout the UI.
summary.outcome: Full MUI migration completed with 12 new React components, light theme applied, token chart fixed by separating cache_read into its own chart and switching to grouped bars, 'checkpoint' renamed to 'commit' in all user-facing text, old Astro components deleted, and all build/test/lint checks passing.

repo: Project is an Astro site with a Bitcoin page (/) that must remain untouched alongside the AI metrics page (/ai-metrics).
Token data has a severe skew: cache_read tokens represent 93-98% of total tokens, making input/output/cache_creation invisible in a stacked chart.
The codebase previously used a dark theme with DARK_CHART_OPTIONS defined in ai-metrics-utils.ts.
Internal data identifiers use 'checkpoint' but user-facing labels were changed to 'commit' — the data schema was not modified.
There are 159 tests in the project that all passed after the migration.

code.finding: Initial implementation used innerHTML to clear the chart container, which triggered a warning; fixed to use safe DOM method instead.
Token Breakdown chart (Metric 6) excludes cache_read from the grouped bar chart and references the Cache Leverage chart (Metric 7) for that data instead.
MUI theme uses white Paper, blue primary, purple secondary for the light theme.
Layout background changed to #f5f5f5 for the light theme.
DARK_CHART_OPTIONS export was removed; file retained for other utilities used by the data layer.

workflow: Parallel agent spawning (3 simultaneous Explore agents) was used for initial codebase analysis to save time.
React islands via client:load is the Astro pattern used to hydrate MUI components — the entire dashboard is a single React island.
Dev server startup was verified separately from build to catch runtime-only errors.
Phased task breakdown (Phase 0–6) with TodoWrite tracking kept the large migration organized.

friction: LightweightChartWrapper initially used innerHTML for DOM cleanup, causing a warning that required a fix before proceeding.
Two attempts were needed to start the dev server and check its output (ran the check command twice).
The astro.config.mjs required two separate edits, suggesting the first edit was incomplete or incorrect.
open_items: The cache_read token value is now only visible in the Cache Leverage chart (Metric 7) — it may be worth adding a callout or annotation on the Token Breakdown chart to make this discoverability more obvious to users.
Internal identifiers still use 'checkpoint' to match the data schema — if the data source is ever updated, a coordinated rename would be needed.
No visual screenshot or browser verification was performed — only build/test/lint validation confirmed correctness.
